# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |
| Context name empty | `ContextNameError::Empty` | Context name cannot be blank |
| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete next action) | `DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Next action not found (edit) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Invalid title (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_next_action_title service |
| Inbox item not found (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
| Not authorized (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
| Invalid title (add project) | `AddProjectError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_project service |
| Project not found (complete) | `CompleteProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (complete project) | `CompleteProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Project not found (delete) | `DeleteProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete project) | `DeleteProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Project not found (edit) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit project) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Invalid title (edit project) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_project_title service |
| Project not found (add action) | `AddProjectNextActionError::ProjectNotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (add project action) | `AddProjectNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Inbox item not found (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
| Not authorized (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
| Invalid first action title (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | First action title validation failure in clarify_as_project service |
| Inbox item not found (clarify as waiting for) | `ClarifyAsWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
| Not authorized (clarify as waiting for) | `ClarifyAsWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
| Invalid waiting-on (clarify as waiting for) | `ClarifyAsWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError)` | Waiting-on validation failure in clarify_as_waiting_for service |
| Waiting-on empty | `WaitingOnError::Empty` | Waiting-on cannot be blank |
| Waiting-on too long | `WaitingOnError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Waiting-on exceeds 100 character limit |
| Invalid title (add waiting-for) | `AddWaitingForError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_waiting_for_item service |
| Invalid waiting-on (add waiting-for) | `AddWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError)` | Waiting-on validation failure in add_waiting_for_item service |
| Waiting-for not found (resolve) | `ResolveWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
| Not authorized (resolve waiting-for) | `ResolveWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
| Waiting-for not found (delete) | `DeleteWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete waiting-for) | `DeleteWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
| Waiting-for not found (edit) | `UpdateWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
| Invalid title (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_waiting_for_item service |
| Invalid waiting-on (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError)` | Waiting-on validation failure in update_waiting_for_item service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add waiting-for) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit waiting-for) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (waiting-for) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
| `WaitingOnError::Empty` (clarify as waiting for) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `WaitingOnError::TooLong` (clarify as waiting for) | "That name is too long (max 100 characters)" |
| `WaitingOnError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `WaitingOnError::Empty` (edit) | "Who or what cannot be empty" |
| `WaitingOnError::TooLong` | "That name is too long (max 100 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

---

## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)

*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
implementation.*

### Core GTD Concepts

| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. **Implemented.** |
| Project ID | `ProjectId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a project. **Implemented.** |
| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` (enum: Active, Resolved) | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on, and date added. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item ID | `WaitingForId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a waiting-for item. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting-on | `WaitingOn` | Non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed. Who or what the user is waiting on. Validated at construction via `WaitingOn::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |

### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction. **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as waiting for | `clarify_as_waiting_for()` | InboxItem -> WaitingForItem (requires waiting_on). **Implemented.** |
| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem (not yet implemented as clarify action) |
| Add waiting-for item | `add_waiting_for_item()` | Service: creates WaitingForItem (Active) with title and waiting_on person. **Implemented.** |
| Mark received (resolve waiting-for) | `WaitingForItem::resolve()` | Active -> Resolved; records resolution time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete waiting-for item | `delete_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit waiting-for item | `update_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title and person, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add project | `add_project()` | Service: creates Project (Active) with title. **Implemented.** |
| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Drop project | `Project::drop_project()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned. **Implemented.** |
| Delete project | `delete_project()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit project title | `update_project_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action to project | `add_next_action_to_project()` | Service: creates NextAction linked to project. **Implemented.** |
| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
| Resolve waiting for | `resolve_waiting_for_item()` | WaitingForItem -> Resolved (marked as received). **Implemented.** |
| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |

### GTD State Machines

**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
```
                capture()
  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
                                │
                      clarify() │
              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
              v        v        v        v        v
         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
```

**NextAction Lifecycle:**
```
                  clarify_as_next_action()
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
                                               │
                                   complete()  │
                                               v
                                         NextAction (Completed)
```

**Project Lifecycle:**
```
                    clarify_as_project()
  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
                                           │  │
                              complete()   │  │  drop()
                                           v  v
                                    Completed / Dropped
```

**WaitingForItem Lifecycle:**
```
              add_waiting_for_item()
  [nothing] ───────────────────────> WaitingForItem (Active)
                                         ^       │
        clarify_as_waiting_for()         │       │
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────┘       │  resolve()  (mark as received)
                                                 v
                                           WaitingForItem (Resolved)
```

Both Active and Resolved variants can be deleted (`delete_waiting_for_item()`).
The `update_waiting_for_item()` action applies to Active items.

file.content:
- Each item shows: what is being waited on, who/what it's waiting on, date added
- User can add items directly to Waiting For
- User can mark a Waiting For item as received (moves to inbox or completes)
- User can convert a Waiting For item to a Next Action
- Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances

**Success Metric:** User can see everything they're waiting on in one glance.

### US-GTD-6: Someday/Maybe List

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** park ideas and someday projects,
**So that** they don't clutter my action lists but aren't forgotten either.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Someday/Maybe list shows all parked ideas
- User can add items directly to Someday/Maybe
- User can activate an item (moves to inbox for clarification, or directly to Next Actions)
- User can delete items that are no longer interesting
- List is reviewed during the weekly review
- Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances

**Success Metric:** Ideas have a home that is not the user's head or their active lists.

### US-GTD-7: Weekly Review

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** be guided through a weekly review process,
**So that** I can keep my system current and trustworthy.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Weekly Review is accessible from the main navigation
- Review follows three phases:
  1. **Get Clear**: Shows inbox count, prompts user to process to zero
  2. **Get Current**: Shows each active project; user confirms next action exists or adds one.
     Shows Waiting For items; user confirms still waiting or resolves. Shows Next Actions;
     user confirms still relevant or removes.
  3. **Get Creative**: Shows Someday/Maybe list for review. Prompts for any new ideas.
- Each phase can be completed independently
- Review progress is tracked (which phases are done)
- Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances

**Success Metric:** A weekly review can be completed in under 30 minutes.

### US-GTD-8: Navigation and GTD Dashboard

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** navigate between Inbox, Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For, and
Someday/Maybe,
**So that** I can access any part of my GTD system quickly.

content.text: Let me look at the WaitingForItem domain type as a reference pattern for how to build SomedayMaybeItem (similar simple domain type).

content.content:
1→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     2→use sqlx::{PgExecutor, PgPool};
     3→use uuid::Uuid;
     4→
     5→use crate::domain::{TodoTitle, UserId, WaitingForId, WaitingForItem, WaitingOn};
     6→
     7→struct WaitingForRecord {
     8→    id: Uuid,
     9→    user_id: Uuid,
    10→    title: String,
    11→    waiting_on: String,
    12→    created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    13→    resolved_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    14→}
    15→
    16→impl WaitingForRecord {
    17→    fn into_domain(self) -> WaitingForItem {
    18→        let id = WaitingForId::from_uuid(self.id);
    19→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);
    20→        let title = TodoTitle::parse(self.title).expect("stored title should be valid");
    21→        let waiting_on =
    22→            WaitingOn::parse(self.waiting_on).expect("stored waiting_on should be valid");
    23→
    24→        WaitingForItem::from_parts(
    25→            id,
    26→            user_id,
    27→            title,
    28→            waiting_on,
    29→            self.created_at,
    30→            self.resolved_at,
    31→        )
    32→    }
    33→}
    34→
    35→pub async fn insert_waiting_for_item(
    36→    executor: impl PgExecutor<'_>,
    37→    item: &WaitingForItem,
    38→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    39→    let resolved_at: Option<&DateTime<Utc>> = match item {
    40→        WaitingForItem::Resolved { resolved_at, .. } => Some(resolved_at),
    41→        WaitingForItem::Active { .. } => None,
    42→    };
    43→
    44→    sqlx::query!(
    45→        r#"INSERT INTO waiting_for_items (id, user_id, title, waiting_on, created_at, resolved_at)
    46→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5 :: timestamptz, $6 :: timestamptz)"#,
    47→        item.id().as_uuid(),
    48→        item.user_id().as_uuid(),
    49→        item.title().as_str(),
    50→        item.waiting_on().as_str(),
    51→        item.created_at() as &DateTime<Utc>,
    52→        resolved_at as Option<&DateTime<Utc>>,
    53→    )
    54→    .execute(executor)
    55→    .await?;
    56→    Ok(())
    57→}
    58→
    59→pub async fn find_active_waiting_for_items_by_user(
    60→    pool: &PgPool,
    61→    user_id: &UserId,
    62→) -> Result<Vec<WaitingForItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    63→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    64→        WaitingForRecord,
    65→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title, waiting_on,
    66→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    67→           resolved_at as "resolved_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    68→           FROM waiting_for_items
    69→           WHERE user_id = $1 AND resolved_at IS NULL
    70→           ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    71→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    72→    )
    73→    .fetch_all(pool)
    74→    .await?;
    75→
    76→    Ok(records.into_iter().map(|r| r.into_domain()).collect())
    77→}
    78→
    79→pub async fn find_waiting_for_item_by_id(
    80→    pool: &PgPool,
    81→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
    82→) -> Result<Option<WaitingForItem>, sqlx::Error> {
    83→    let record: Option<WaitingForRecord> = sqlx::query_as!(
    84→        WaitingForRecord,
    85→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, title, waiting_on,
    86→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    87→           resolved_at as "resolved_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    88→           FROM waiting_for_items WHERE id = $1"#,
    89→        item_id.as_uuid(),
    90→    )
    91→    .fetch_optional(pool)
    92→    .await?;
    93→
    94→    Ok(record.map(|r| r.into_domain()))
    95→}
    96→
    97→pub async fn resolve_waiting_for_item(
    98→    pool: &PgPool,
    99→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
   100→    resolved_at: &DateTime<Utc>,
   101→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   102→    sqlx::query!(
   103→        r#"UPDATE waiting_for_items SET resolved_at = $1 :: timestamptz WHERE id = $2"#,
   104→        resolved_at as &DateTime<Utc>,
   105→        item_id.as_uuid(),
   106→    )
   107→    .execute(pool)
   108→    .await?;
   109→    Ok(())
   110→}
   111→
   112→pub async fn delete_waiting_for_item(
   113→    pool: &PgPool,
   114→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
   115→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   116→    sqlx::query!(
   117→        r#"DELETE FROM waiting_for_items WHERE id = $1"#,
   118→        item_id.as_uuid(),
   119→    )
   120→    .execute(pool)
   121→    .await?;
   122→    Ok(())
   123→}
   124→
   125→pub async fn count_active_waiting_for_items(
   126→    pool: &PgPool,
   127→    user_id: &UserId,
   128→) -> Result<i64, sqlx::Error> {
   129→    let record = sqlx::query!(
   130→        r#"SELECT COUNT(*) as "count!" FROM waiting_for_items WHERE user_id = $1 AND resolved_at IS NULL"#,
   131→        user_id.as_uuid(),
   132→    )
   133→    .fetch_one(pool)
   134→    .await?;
   135→
   136→    Ok(record.count)
   137→}
   138→
   139→pub async fn update_waiting_for_item(
   140→    pool: &PgPool,
   141→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
   142→    title: &TodoTitle,
   143→    waiting_on: &WaitingOn,
   144→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   145→    sqlx::query!(
   146→        r#"UPDATE waiting_for_items SET title = $1, waiting_on = $2 WHERE id = $3"#,
   147→        title.as_str(),
   148→        waiting_on.as_str(),
   149→        item_id.as_uuid(),
   150→    )
   151→    .execute(pool)
   152→    .await?;
   153→    Ok(())
   154→}
   155→

1→use axum::Router;
     2→use sqlx::postgres::PgPoolOptions;
     3→use sqlx::PgPool;
     4→use tokio::net::TcpListener;
     5→use tower_http::request_id::{MakeRequestUuid, PropagateRequestIdLayer, SetRequestIdLayer};
     6→use tower_http::services::ServeDir;
     7→use tower_http::trace::TraceLayer;
     8→use tower_sessions::SessionManagerLayer;
     9→use tower_sessions_sqlx_store::PostgresStore;
    10→
    11→use crate::configuration::Settings;
    12→use crate::routes::{
    13→    get_contexts, get_dashboard, get_edit_context, get_edit_next_action, get_edit_project,
    14→    get_edit_todo, get_edit_waiting_for, get_forgot_password, get_inbox, get_login,
    15→    get_next_action_item, get_next_actions, get_project_detail, get_project_item, get_projects,
    16→    get_register, get_review, get_someday_maybe, get_todo_item, get_todos_page, get_waiting_for,
    17→    get_waiting_for_item, health_check, index, post_clarify_inbox_item, post_complete_next_action,
    18→    post_complete_project, post_complete_waiting_for, post_context, post_delete_context,
    19→    post_delete_inbox_item, post_delete_next_action, post_delete_project, post_delete_todo,
    20→    post_delete_waiting_for, post_edit_context, post_edit_next_action, post_edit_project,
    21→    post_edit_todo, post_edit_waiting_for, post_inbox, post_login, post_logout, post_next_action,
    22→    post_project, post_project_next_action, post_register, post_todo, post_toggle_todo,
    23→    post_waiting_for,
    24→};
    25→
    26→pub struct Application {
    27→    listener: TcpListener,
    28→    pool: PgPool,
    29→}
    30→
    31→impl Application {
    32→    pub async fn build(settings: &Settings) -> Result<Self, anyhow::Error> {
    33→        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()
    34→            .max_connections(5)
    35→            .connect_with(settings.database.connect_options())
    36→            .await?;
    37→
    38→        sqlx::migrate!("./migrations").run(&pool).await?;
    39→
    40→        let session_store = PostgresStore::new(pool.clone());
    41→        session_store.migrate().await?;
    42→
    43→        let address = format!(
    44→            "{}:{}",
    45→            settings.application.host, settings.application.port
    46→        );
    47→        let listener = TcpListener::bind(&address).await?;
    48→
    49→        tracing::info!("Listening on {}", listener.local_addr()?);
    50→
    51→        Ok(Self { listener, pool })
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    pub fn port(&self) -> u16 {
    55→        self.listener.local_addr().unwrap().port()
    56→    }
    57→
    58→    pub fn router(pool: PgPool) -> Router {
    59→        let x_request_id = axum::http::HeaderName::from_static("x-request-id");
    60→
    61→        let session_store = PostgresStore::new(pool.clone());
    62→        let session_layer = SessionManagerLayer::new(session_store);
    63→
    64→        Router::new()
    65→            .route("/", axum::routing::get(index))
    66→            .route("/dashboard", axum::routing::get(get_dashboard))
    67→            .route("/inbox", axum::routing::get(get_inbox).post(post_inbox))
    68→            .route(
    69→                "/inbox/{id}/delete",
    70→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_inbox_item),
    71→            )
    72→            .route(
    73→                "/inbox/{id}/clarify",
    74→                axum::routing::post(post_clarify_inbox_item),
    75→            )
    76→            .route(
    77→                "/contexts",
    78→                axum::routing::get(get_contexts).post(post_context),
    79→            )
    80→            .route(
    81→                "/contexts/{id}/edit",
    82→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_context).post(post_edit_context),
    83→            )
    84→            .route(
    85→                "/contexts/{id}/delete",
    86→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_context),
    87→            )
    88→            .route(
    89→                "/next-actions",
    90→                axum::routing::get(get_next_actions).post(post_next_action),
    91→            )
    92→            .route(
    93→                "/next-actions/{id}",
    94→                axum::routing::get(get_next_action_item),
    95→            )
    96→            .route(
    97→                "/next-actions/{id}/complete",
    98→                axum::routing::post(post_complete_next_action),
    99→            )
   100→            .route(
   101→                "/next-actions/{id}/delete",
   102→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_next_action),
   103→            )
   104→            .route(
   105→                "/next-actions/{id}/edit",
   106→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_next_action).post(post_edit_next_action),
   107→            )
   108→            .route(
   109→                "/projects",
   110→                axum::routing::get(get_projects).post(post_project),
   111→            )
   112→            .route("/projects/{id}", axum::routing::get(get_project_detail))
   113→            .route("/projects/{id}/item", axum::routing::get(get_project_item))
   114→            .route(
   115→                "/projects/{id}/complete",
   116→                axum::routing::post(post_complete_project),
   117→            )
   118→            .route(
   119→                "/projects/{id}/delete",
   120→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_project),
   121→            )
   122→            .route(
   123→                "/projects/{id}/edit",
   124→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_project).post(post_edit_project),
   125→            )
   126→            .route(
   127→                "/projects/{id}/next-actions",
   128→                axum::routing::post(post_project_next_action),
   129→            )
   130→            .route(
   131→                "/waiting-for",
   132→                axum::routing::get(get_waiting_for).post(post_waiting_for),
   133→            )
   134→            .route(
   135→                "/waiting-for/{id}",
   136→                axum::routing::get(get_waiting_for_item),
   137→            )
   138→            .route(
   139→                "/waiting-for/{id}/complete",
   140→                axum::routing::post(post_complete_waiting_for),
   141→            )
   142→            .route(
   143→                "/waiting-for/{id}/delete",
   144→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_waiting_for),
   145→            )
   146→            .route(
   147→                "/waiting-for/{id}/edit",
   148→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_waiting_for).post(post_edit_waiting_for),
   149→            )
   150→            .route("/someday-maybe", axum::routing::get(get_someday_maybe))
   151→            .route("/review", axum::routing::get(get_review))
   152→            .route(
   153→                "/register",
   154→                axum::routing::get(get_register).post(post_register),
   155→            )
   156→            .route("/login", axum::routing::get(get_login).post(post_login))
   157→            .route("/forgot-password", axum::routing::get(get_forgot_password))
   158→            .route("/logout", axum::routing::post(post_logout))
   159→            .route("/todos", axum::routing::get(get_todos_page).post(post_todo))
   160→            .route("/todos/{id}", axum::routing::get(get_todo_item))
   161→            .route("/todos/{id}/toggle", axum::routing::post(post_toggle_todo))
   162→            .route("/todos/{id}/delete", axum::routing::post(post_delete_todo))
   163→            .route(
   164→                "/todos/{id}/edit",
   165→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_todo).post(post_edit_todo),
   166→            )
   167→            .route("/health_check", axum::routing::get(health_check))
   168→            .nest_service("/static", ServeDir::new("static"))
   169→            .layer(session_layer)
   170→            .layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http())
   171→            .layer(PropagateRequestIdLayer::new(x_request_id.clone()))
   172→            .layer(SetRequestIdLayer::new(x_request_id, MakeRequestUuid))
   173→            .with_state(pool)
   174→    }
   175→
   176→    pub async fn run(self) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
   177→        let router = Self::router(self.pool);
   178→        axum::serve(self.listener, router).await
   179→    }
   180→}
   181→

content.text: Now let me also check the existing services and the routes mod.rs for exports.
Let me also check the waiting_for_service to see the service pattern, and the CSS to understand the styling structure.

content.content:
1→use sqlx::PgPool;
     2→
     3→use crate::domain::{
     4→    TodoTitle, TodoTitleError, UserId, WaitingForId, WaitingForItem, WaitingOn, WaitingOnError,
     5→};
     6→use crate::infrastructure::waiting_for_repository;
     7→
     8→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
     9→pub enum AddWaitingForError {
    10→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
    11→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
    12→    #[error("Invalid waiting-on: {0}")]
    13→    InvalidWaitingOn(#[from] WaitingOnError),
    14→    #[error(transparent)]
    15→    Unexpected(anyhow::Error),
    16→}
    17→
    18→#[tracing::instrument(
    19→    name = "add_waiting_for_item",
    20→    skip(pool, title, waiting_on),
    21→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    22→)]
    23→pub async fn add_waiting_for_item(
    24→    pool: &PgPool,
    25→    user_id: UserId,
    26→    title: String,
    27→    waiting_on: String,
    28→) -> Result<WaitingForItem, AddWaitingForError> {
    29→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(title)?;
    30→    let waiting_on = WaitingOn::parse(waiting_on)?;
    31→    let item = WaitingForItem::new(user_id, title, waiting_on);
    32→
    33→    waiting_for_repository::insert_waiting_for_item(pool, &item)
    34→        .await
    35→        .map_err(|e| {
    36→            AddWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
    37→                "Failed to insert waiting-for item: {e}"
    38→            ))
    39→        })?;
    40→
    41→    tracing::info!("Waiting-for item added");
    42→    Ok(item)
    43→}
    44→
    45→#[tracing::instrument(
    46→    name = "list_active_waiting_for_items",
    47→    skip(pool),
    48→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    49→)]
    50→pub async fn list_active_waiting_for_items(
    51→    pool: &PgPool,
    52→    user_id: &UserId,
    53→) -> Result<Vec<WaitingForItem>, anyhow::Error> {
    54→    let items = waiting_for_repository::find_active_waiting_for_items_by_user(pool, user_id)
    55→        .await
    56→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to fetch waiting-for items: {e}"))?;
    57→
    58→    Ok(items)
    59→}
    60→
    61→#[tracing::instrument(
    62→    name = "count_active_waiting_for_items",
    63→    skip(pool),
    64→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    65→)]
    66→pub async fn count_active_waiting_for_items(
    67→    pool: &PgPool,
    68→    user_id: &UserId,
    69→) -> Result<i64, anyhow::Error> {
    70→    let count = waiting_for_repository::count_active_waiting_for_items(pool, user_id)
    71→        .await
    72→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to count waiting-for items: {e}"))?;
    73→
    74→    Ok(count)
    75→}
    76→
    77→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
    78→pub enum ResolveWaitingForError {
    79→    #[error("Waiting-for item not found")]
    80→    NotFound,
    81→    #[error("Not authorized to resolve this waiting-for item")]
    82→    Unauthorized,
    83→    #[error(transparent)]
    84→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    85→}
    86→
    87→#[tracing::instrument(
    88→    name = "resolve_waiting_for_item",
    89→    skip(pool),
    90→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    91→)]
    92→pub async fn resolve_waiting_for_item(
    93→    pool: &PgPool,
    94→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
    95→    user_id: &UserId,
    96→) -> Result<(), ResolveWaitingForError> {
    97→    let item = waiting_for_repository::find_waiting_for_item_by_id(pool, item_id)
    98→        .await
    99→        .map_err(|e| ResolveWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   100→        .ok_or(ResolveWaitingForError::NotFound)?;
   101→
   102→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
   103→        return Err(ResolveWaitingForError::Unauthorized);
   104→    }
   105→
   106→    let resolved = item.resolve();
   107→    if let WaitingForItem::Resolved { resolved_at, .. } = &resolved {
   108→        waiting_for_repository::resolve_waiting_for_item(pool, item_id, resolved_at)
   109→            .await
   110→            .map_err(|e| {
   111→                ResolveWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   112→                    "Failed to resolve waiting-for item: {e}"
   113→                ))
   114→            })?;
   115→    }
   116→
   117→    tracing::info!("Waiting-for item resolved");
   118→    Ok(())
   119→}
   120→
   121→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   122→pub enum DeleteWaitingForError {
   123→    #[error("Waiting-for item not found")]
   124→    NotFound,
   125→    #[error("Not authorized to delete this waiting-for item")]
   126→    Unauthorized,
   127→    #[error(transparent)]
   128→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   129→}
   130→
   131→#[tracing::instrument(
   132→    name = "delete_waiting_for_item",
   133→    skip(pool),
   134→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   135→)]
   136→pub async fn delete_waiting_for_item(
   137→    pool: &PgPool,
   138→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
   139→    user_id: &UserId,
   140→) -> Result<(), DeleteWaitingForError> {
   141→    let item = waiting_for_repository::find_waiting_for_item_by_id(pool, item_id)
   142→        .await
   143→        .map_err(|e| DeleteWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   144→        .ok_or(DeleteWaitingForError::NotFound)?;
   145→
   146→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
   147→        return Err(DeleteWaitingForError::Unauthorized);
   148→    }
   149→
   150→    waiting_for_repository::delete_waiting_for_item(pool, item_id)
   151→        .await
   152→        .map_err(|e| {
   153→            DeleteWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   154→                "Failed to delete waiting-for item: {e}"
   155→            ))
   156→        })?;
   157→
   158→    tracing::info!("Waiting-for item deleted");
   159→    Ok(())
   160→}
   161→
   162→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   163→pub enum UpdateWaitingForError {
   164→    #[error("Waiting-for item not found")]
   165→    NotFound,
   166→    #[error("Not authorized to edit this waiting-for item")]
   167→    Unauthorized,
   168→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
   169→    InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError),
   170→    #[error("Invalid waiting-on: {0}")]
   171→    InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError),
   172→    #[error(transparent)]
   173→    Unexpected(anyhow::Error),
   174→}
   175→
   176→#[tracing::instrument(
   177→    name = "update_waiting_for_item",
   178→    skip(pool, new_title, new_waiting_on),
   179→    fields(item_id = %item_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   180→)]
   181→pub async fn update_waiting_for_item(
   182→    pool: &PgPool,
   183→    item_id: &WaitingForId,
   184→    user_id: &UserId,
   185→    new_title: String,
   186→    new_waiting_on: String,
   187→) -> Result<(), UpdateWaitingForError> {
   188→    let item = waiting_for_repository::find_waiting_for_item_by_id(pool, item_id)
   189→        .await
   190→        .map_err(|e| UpdateWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   191→        .ok_or(UpdateWaitingForError::NotFound)?;
   192→
   193→    if item.user_id() != user_id {
   194→        return Err(UpdateWaitingForError::Unauthorized);
   195→    }
   196→
   197→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(new_title).map_err(UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidTitle)?;
   198→    let waiting_on =
   199→        WaitingOn::parse(new_waiting_on).map_err(UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn)?;
   200→
   201→    waiting_for_repository::update_waiting_for_item(pool, item_id, &title, &waiting_on)
   202→        .await
   203→        .map_err(|e| {
   204→            UpdateWaitingForError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   205→                "Failed to update waiting-for item: {e}"
   206→            ))
   207→        })?;
   208→
   209→    tracing::info!("Waiting-for item updated");
   210→    Ok(())
   211→}
   212→

content.text: I have a thorough understanding of the codebase patterns now. Let me check what CSS is relevant for the waiting-for section so I can mirror it for someday-maybe.

Good. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me now broadcast the design discussion to the team and begin implementing. Based on the US-GTD-6 acceptance criteria and the existing patterns in the codebase, the Someday/Maybe feature is straightforward: it's a simple list type (simpler than WaitingFor since it has no state machine -- it's just a parked idea with title and created_at).

Let me send my design proposal to the reviewers.

input.content:
Team, I'm the Driver for Step 10: Someday/Maybe (US-GTD-6). I've reviewed the codebase, user story, and glossary thoroughly. Here is my design proposal for consensus:

## Step 10: Someday/Maybe List -- Design Proposal

### Domain Type: `SomedayMaybeItem`
- Simple struct (not an enum). No state machine needed -- a parked idea is just a title + created_at. There's no "completed" or "resolved" state. When you activate it, it moves to inbox (becomes an InboxItem). When you're done with it, you delete it.
- Fields: `id: SomedayMaybeId`, `user_id: UserId`, `title: TodoTitle`, `created_at: DateTime<Utc>`
- `SomedayMaybeId(Uuid)` newtype

### Database
- New migration: `20240101000009_create_someday_maybe_items_table.sql`
- Table: `someday_maybe_items` (id, user_id, title, created_at)
- FK to users table

### Repository: `someday_maybe_repository.rs`
- `insert_someday_maybe_item(executor, &item)` -- PgExecutor for transaction support
- `find_someday_maybe_items_by_user(pool, user_id)` -- all items, ordered by created_at ASC
- `find_someday_maybe_item_by_id(pool, item_id)` -- single item
- `delete_someday_maybe_item(pool, item_id)` -- permanent removal
- `count_someday_maybe_items(pool, user_id)` -- for future nav badge
- `update_someday_maybe_title(pool, item_id, title)` -- for edit

### Service: `someday_maybe_service.rs`
- `add_someday_maybe_item(pool, user_id, title)` -> `AddSomedayMaybeError`
- `list_someday_maybe_items(pool, user_id)` -> `Vec<SomedayMaybeItem>`
- `delete_someday_maybe_item(pool, item_id, user_id)` -> `DeleteSomedayMaybeError`
- `update_someday_maybe_title(pool, item_id, user_id, title)` -> `UpdateSomedayMaybeError`
- `activate_someday_maybe_item(pool, item_id, user_id)` -> `ActivateSomedayMaybeError`
  - This moves the item to inbox: begins TX, finds item, checks ownership, creates InboxItem with same title, deletes someday/maybe item, commits

### Routes: Replace `get_someday_maybe` placeholder in `gtd_placeholders.rs`
- New file: `routes/someday_maybe.rs`
- `GET /someday-maybe` -- list page
- `POST /someday-maybe` -- add new item
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/delete` -- delete item
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` -- inline edit form (HTMX)
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/edit` -- submit edit
- `POST /someday-maybe/{id}/activate` -- move to inbox
- `GET /someday-maybe/{id}` -- single item (HTMX swap target)

### Templates
- `someday_maybe.html` -- full list page (mirrors waiting_for.html pattern)
- `someday_maybe_item.html` -- single item (with Edit, Activate, Delete actions)
- `someday_maybe_edit.html` -- inline edit form

### UI Pattern
- Same pattern as Waiting For: heading, add form, item list
- Each item shows: title, date added
- Actions: Edit (inline), Activate (moves to inbox), Delete
- "Activate" button is the primary action (equivalent to "Received" on WF)
- HTMX: hx-post for add appends to list, hx-post for delete/activate swaps outerHTML to empty

### CSS
- BEM classes: `.someday-maybe`, `.someday-maybe__add`, `.someday-maybe-item`, etc.
- Mirror the waiting-for component structure and token usage
- Simpler than WF since no "person" field, just title

### Activate Flow
- "Activate" creates a new InboxItem with the same title and deletes the SomedayMaybeItem
- User-facing announce: "Moved to Inbox"
- This follows the cross-aggregate service pattern from clarify_as_*

### Error Copy
- Empty title on add: silently ignored (consistent with other add forms)
- Empty title on edit: "Title cannot be empty"
- Too long title: "That title is too long (max 300 characters)"

### Glossary Updates
- Add `SomedayMaybeItem`, `SomedayMaybeId`, activate action, error types
- Mark as Implemented

### Not In Scope (Step 11 handles this)
- Clarify as Someday/Maybe (inbox -> someday/maybe) is Step 11, not Step 10

Please review and provide feedback. I'll start implementation once we have consensus on the design.
input.summary: Step 10 Someday/Maybe design proposal for review

    14→impl InboxRecord {
    15→    fn into_domain(self) -> InboxItem {
    16→        let id = InboxItemId::from_uuid(self.id);
    17→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);

    22→}
    23→

    25→    sqlx::query!(
    26→        r#"INSERT INTO inbox_items (id, user_id, title, created_at)
    27→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4 :: timestamptz)"#,
    28→        item.id().as_uuid(),

content.text:
All 11 Playwright tests pass. Now let me do the refactor check -- look for duplication or naming issues.

The code follows the established patterns closely. I notice the `register_and_login` helper is duplicated across test files (waiting_for.rs, someday_maybe.rs, gtd_placeholders.rs, etc.) -- this was previously noted as a deferred item. I won't fix it now since it's pre-existing and not part of this step's scope.

Let me also check if the `PgPool` import in inbox_repository.rs is still needed (since we changed `insert_inbox_item` to use `PgExecutor`).

`PgPool` is still used by `find_inbox_items_by_user`, `count_inbox_items`, and `find_inbox_item_by_id` uses `PgExecutor` already. So the import is correct.

Now let me update the glossary.

### Core GTD Concepts

| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. **Implemented.** |
| Project ID | `ProjectId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a project. **Implemented.** |
| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` (enum: Active, Resolved) | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on, and date added. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item ID | `WaitingForId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a waiting-for item. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting-on | `WaitingOn` | Non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed. Who or what the user is waiting on. Validated at construction via `WaitingOn::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |

### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction. **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as waiting for | `clarify_as_waiting_for()` | InboxItem -> WaitingForItem (requires waiting_on). **Implemented.** |
| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem (not yet implemented as clarify action) |
| Add waiting-for item | `add_waiting_for_item()` | Service: creates WaitingForItem (Active) with title and waiting_on person. **Implemented.** |
| Mark received (resolve waiting-for) | `WaitingForItem::resolve()` | Active -> Resolved; records resolution time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete waiting-for item | `delete_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit waiting-for item | `update_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title and person, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add project | `add_project()` | Service: creates Project (Active) with title. **Implemented.** |
| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Drop project | `Project::drop_project()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned. **Implemented.** |
| Delete project | `delete_project()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit project title | `update_project_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action to project | `add_next_action_to_project()` | Service: creates NextAction linked to project. **Implemented.** |
| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
| Resolve waiting for | `resolve_waiting_for_item()` | WaitingForItem -> Resolved (marked as received). **Implemented.** |
| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |

### GTD State Machines

**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
```
                capture()
  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
                                │
                      clarify() │
              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
              v        v        v        v        v
         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
```

**NextAction Lifecycle:**
```
                  clarify_as_next_action()
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
                                               │
                                   complete()  │
                                               v
                                         NextAction (Completed)
```

**Project Lifecycle:**
```
                    clarify_as_project()
  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
                                           │  │
                              complete()   │  │  drop()
                                           v  v
                                    Completed / Dropped
```

**WaitingForItem Lifecycle:**
```
              add_waiting_for_item()
  [nothing] ───────────────────────> WaitingForItem (Active)
                                         ^       │
        clarify_as_waiting_for()         │       │
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────┘       │  resolve()  (mark as received)
                                                 v
                                           WaitingForItem (Resolved)
```

Both Active and Resolved variants can be deleted (`delete_waiting_for_item()`).
The `update_waiting_for_item()` action applies to Active items.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |
| Context name empty | `ContextNameError::Empty` | Context name cannot be blank |
| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete next action) | `DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Next action not found (edit) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Invalid title (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_next_action_title service |
| Inbox item not found (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
| Not authorized (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
| Invalid title (add project) | `AddProjectError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_project service |
| Project not found (complete) | `CompleteProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (complete project) | `CompleteProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Project not found (delete) | `DeleteProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete project) | `DeleteProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Project not found (edit) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit project) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Invalid title (edit project) | `UpdateProjectTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_project_title service |
| Project not found (add action) | `AddProjectNextActionError::ProjectNotFound` | Referenced project does not exist |
| Not authorized (add project action) | `AddProjectNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the project |
| Inbox item not found (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
| Not authorized (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
| Invalid first action title (clarify as project) | `ClarifyAsProjectError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | First action title validation failure in clarify_as_project service |
| Inbox item not found (clarify as waiting for) | `ClarifyAsWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
| Not authorized (clarify as waiting for) | `ClarifyAsWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
| Invalid waiting-on (clarify as waiting for) | `ClarifyAsWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError)` | Waiting-on validation failure in clarify_as_waiting_for service |
| Waiting-on empty | `WaitingOnError::Empty` | Waiting-on cannot be blank |
| Waiting-on too long | `WaitingOnError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Waiting-on exceeds 100 character limit |
| Invalid title (add waiting-for) | `AddWaitingForError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_waiting_for_item service |
| Invalid waiting-on (add waiting-for) | `AddWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError)` | Waiting-on validation failure in add_waiting_for_item service |
| Waiting-for not found (resolve) | `ResolveWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
| Not authorized (resolve waiting-for) | `ResolveWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
| Waiting-for not found (delete) | `DeleteWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete waiting-for) | `DeleteWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
| Waiting-for not found (edit) | `UpdateWaitingForError::NotFound` | Referenced waiting-for item does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the waiting-for item |
| Invalid title (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_waiting_for_item service |
| Invalid waiting-on (edit waiting-for) | `UpdateWaitingForError::InvalidWaitingOn(WaitingOnError)` | Waiting-on validation failure in update_waiting_for_item service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add waiting-for) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit waiting-for) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (waiting-for) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
| `WaitingOnError::Empty` (clarify as waiting for) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `WaitingOnError::TooLong` (clarify as waiting for) | "That name is too long (max 100 characters)" |
| `WaitingOnError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `WaitingOnError::Empty` (edit) | "Who or what cannot be empty" |
| `WaitingOnError::TooLong` | "That name is too long (max 100 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

---

## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)

*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
implementation.*

### Core GTD Concepts

| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. **Implemented.** |
| Project ID | `ProjectId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a project. **Implemented.** |
| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` (enum: Active, Resolved) | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on, and date added. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item ID | `WaitingForId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a waiting-for item. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting-on | `WaitingOn` | Non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed. Who or what the user is waiting on. Validated at construction via `WaitingOn::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |

### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction. **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as waiting for | `clarify_as_waiting_for()` | InboxItem -> WaitingForItem (requires waiting_on). **Implemented.** |
| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem (not yet implemented as clarify action) |
| Add waiting-for item | `add_waiting_for_item()` | Service: creates WaitingForItem (Active) with title and waiting_on person. **Implemented.** |
| Mark received (resolve waiting-for) | `WaitingForItem::resolve()` | Active -> Resolved; records resolution time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete waiting-for item | `delete_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit waiting-for item | `update_waiting_for_item()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title and person, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add project | `add_project()` | Service: creates Project (Active) with title. **Implemented.** |
| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Drop project | `Project::drop_project()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned. **Implemented.** |
| Delete project | `delete_project()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit project title | `update_project_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action to project | `add_next_action_to_project()` | Service: creates NextAction linked to project. **Implemented.** |
| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
| Resolve waiting for | `resolve_waiting_for_item()` | WaitingForItem -> Resolved (marked as received). **Implemented.** |
| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |

### GTD State Machines

**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
```
                capture()
  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
                                │
                      clarify() │
              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
              v        v        v        v        v
         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
```

**NextAction Lifecycle:**
```
                  clarify_as_next_action()
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
                                               │
                                   complete()  │
                                               v
                                         NextAction (Completed)
```

**Project Lifecycle:**
```
                    clarify_as_project()
  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
                                           │  │
                              complete()   │  │  drop()
                                           v  v
                                    Completed / Dropped
```

**WaitingForItem Lifecycle:**
```
              add_waiting_for_item()
  [nothing] ───────────────────────> WaitingForItem (Active)
                                         ^       │
        clarify_as_waiting_for()         │       │
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────┘       │  resolve()  (mark as received)
                                                 v
                                           WaitingForItem (Resolved)
```

Both Active and Resolved variants can be deleted (`delete_waiting_for_item()`).
The `update_waiting_for_item()` action applies to Active items.
